established_risk.question: Did the system catch what it's supposed to catch?

emergent_risk.question: Is the system looking for it?

projected_risk.question: Is the system prepared for what's coming?

factors.name: Purpose Limitation
factors.definition: Data used only for stated purpose. No secondary use without justification.

factors.name: Fairness
factors.definition: Age thresholds, accessibility, non-discrimination across protected categories.

factors.name: Consent
factors.definition: User permission obtained before processing. Collection, frequency, withdrawal.

factors.name: Data Collection Minimization
factors.definition: Data collected limited to what is necessary.

factors.name: User Settings & Controls
factors.definition: Product respects existing user choices for privacy and visibility.

factors.name: Notice & Disclosure
factors.definition: Users informed via privacy policies, first-time notices, in-product disclosures.

factors.name: Internal Security
factors.definition: Data protected by access controls, encryption, internal security measures.

factors.name: External Data Sharing
factors.definition: Controls on sharing data with third-party vendors, advertisers, partners.

factors.name: External Data Use
factors.definition: Controls on how external parties use data received from Meta. Anti-scraping.

factors.name: Data Retention
factors.definition: Data retained for defined period with stated justification. Deletion triggers.

factors.name: Deletion
factors.definition: Product respects user and account deletion requests.

factors.name: Audience Visibility
factors.definition: Users understand who can see their data before it becomes visible.

factors.name: Data Creation Minimization
factors.definition: No new data created or derived beyond necessity.

factors.name: User Access & Portability
factors.definition: Users can access, download, and review data collected about them.

factors.name: GenAI Safety
factors.definition: GenAI outputs comply with policies, watermarks, adversarial testing.

factors.name: Integrity & Enforcement
factors.definition: Content reviewed for violations. Users notified and can dispute decisions.

factors.name: Automated Decision-Making
factors.definition: Systems making or influencing decisions about users without human review.

factors.name: Cross-border Transfer
factors.definition: User data moves across jurisdictional boundaries.

factors.name: Profiling & Inference
factors.definition: Deriving new data about users from existing data.
factors.boundary: Evaluates WHETHER new data is created, not whether the underlying use was authorized (see purpose_limitation).

factors.name: Precise Geolocation
factors.definition: Collection or use of precise location data (GPS, Wi-Fi, IP-based).

factors.name: Health & Wellness Data
factors.definition: Collection or processing of health, medical, fitness, or wellness data.

factors.name: Protected Characteristics
factors.definition: Processing racial, ethnic, religious, political, sexual orientation, or genetic data.

factors.name: AI Training Data Governance
factors.definition: Constraints on what data can be used for model training.

factors.name: User Appeals & Recourse
factors.definition: Users can report content, dispute decisions, receive notice of enforcement.

factors.name: EU AI Act Risk Classification
factors.definition: AI systems classified by risk level. High-risk requires conformity assessments.

factors.name: Right to Explanation
factors.definition: Users have right to meaningful info about logic in automated decisions.

factors.name: Data Sovereignty & Localization
factors.definition: Regulations requiring data stored/processed within national boundaries.

factors.name: Algorithmic Auditing
factors.definition: Independent audits of algorithmic systems.

factors.name: Children's Safety by Design
factors.definition: Products accessed by children must implement safety by design.

graduation_thresholds.established: System has built requirements and dedicated category
